diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Isolated fourth ventricle in Chiari II malformation
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with deterioration in motor function, headaches, lethargy, and decline in mobility. MRI revealed an isolated fourth ventricle. The reasoning includes the obstruction of the outlet foraminae of the fourth ventricle, possibly due to scarring, leading to ventricular enlargement. The patient's clinical state and imaging findings supported the diagnosis.
